engineGuide.h2: Vauxhall Vivaro Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Vauxhall Vivaro across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced 2006 as Vivaro's mainstream 2.0 dCi; timing chain tensioner issues on high-mileage commercial use drove replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van 2700, Panel Van 2900, Crew Van, Combi, Platform Cab, L1H1, L2H2
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (60k-100k miles)
EGR valve clogging on short-journey use
Turbo actuator failure on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated 2.0 dCi for Mk2 Vivaro; improved emissions but retained timing chain design requiring verification on commercial fleets.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van 2700/2900/3100, Crew Van, Combi 8/9-seat, Platform Cab, L2H2, L2H3
commonFailures: EGR cooler clogging (40k-80k miles)
DPF regeneration failure on urban use
Injector seal leaks causing rough running
entries.cta: Get quotes for R9M Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Early Mk1 Vivaro workhorse; belt-driven interference engine requiring timely replacement history verification.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van 2700, Crew Van 2700, L1H1, early L2H2
commonFailures: Timing belt failure if not replaced (interference)
Swirl flap actuator sticking
Oil pump wear on high-mileage commercial use
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Heavy-duty option for high-payload Vivaro variants; robust but turbo and intercooler prone to wear on commercial duty.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van 2900/3100, Crew Van heavy-duty, Platform Cab, Chassis Cab
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal leaks
Intercooler hose splitting under load
Injector coding issues after replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for G9U Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L BlueHDi
entries.history: PSA-sourced replacement for Renault units on Mk3; requires AdBlue calibration for emissions compliance on commercial fitment.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van 2700/2900/3100, Crew Van, Combi, Platform Cab, L2H2, L2H3
commonFailures: AdBlue injector crystallisation
NOx sensor failure triggering limp mode
EGR cooler internal leaks causing coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10 Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L BlueHDi
entries.history: Efficient entry-level diesel for Mk3 Vivaro; compact design but EGR/AdBlue systems require specialist calibration on replacement.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van 2700, Crew Van 2700, L1H1, L2H1
commonFailures: EGR cooler recall-related internal leaks
AdBlue tank heater failure in cold weather
Turbo actuator sticking on stop-start use
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5 Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Turbo D High-Output
entries.history: Performance diesel for high-payload Vivaro variants; specialist rebuild recommended to maintain towing and payload ratings.
compatibleVariants: Platform Cab 3100, Chassis Cab 3100, heavy-duty Panel Van conversions
commonFailures: Dual mass flywheel wear under heavy load
High-pressure fuel pump failure
Turbo wastegate actuator calibration drift

commonProblems.h2: Vauxhall Vivaro Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Vauxhall Vivaro's Renault-sourced and PSA-sourced diesel engines are commercial workhorses, but high-mileage use exposes weaknesses in timing components, emissions systems and turbochargers. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your business.

problems.group: Vauxhall Vivaro Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Vauxhall Vivaro Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why M9R/R9M 2.0 dCi Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Vivaro Panel Van, Crew Van, Combi (2006-2019, M9R704, M9R708, R9M404, R9M408, R9M454)
problems.rootCause: Original tensioner design loses pressure under commercial stop-start use, allowing chain slack that damages guides and can jump timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, chain if stretched; oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before rattling; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain kit, inspect valves, reset timing; risk of hidden damage
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix if no valve contact; not recommended for high-mileage commercial use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for commercial operators; minimises downtime and repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a Vivaro used for revenue-generating work, preventative chain service at 60k miles is cost-effective. Once rattling starts, replacement with a rebuilt unit including upgraded tensioner is usually smarter than risking valve damage and extended downtime.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M9R/R9M engine prices for your Vauxhall Vivaro

problems.h4: Vauxhall Vivaro EGR Cooler Failure - Coolant Loss & Overheating on Commercial Diesel Use
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Vivaro Panel Van, Crew Van, Combi (2014-2024, R9M408, R9M454, DV5504, DW10504, DW10508)
problems.rootCause: Internal cooler cracks allow exhaust gases into coolant, causing pressure loss, overheating and potential head gasket damage on high-mileage commercial units.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Blank EGR valve, clean cooler externally, reset fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; does not address internal cooler cracks; may affect emissions compliance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler assembly, flush cooling system, recalibrate emissions
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses symptom but not root cause if engine has suffered overheating damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated cooler design, full diagnostics, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating occurred; prevents repeat failure and protects business uptime

problems.recommendation: If coolant loss is caught early with no overheating, cooler replacement is viable. For Vivaro used in commercial service where downtime costs money, a rebuilt engine with updated components offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for R9M/DW10 Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement with EGR upgrade

problems.h4: Vauxhall Vivaro DPF Regeneration Failure - Urban Use Clogging on Commercial Diesel Variants
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Vivaro Panel Van, Crew Van, Combi (2014-2024, R9M, DV5, DW10)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey commercial use prevents active DPF regeneration; soot accumulation triggers limp mode and can damage turbo or injectors if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, forced regen cycle, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Only works if filter not physically damaged; temporary fix for urban-use vehicles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car clean or new filter fit, sensor calibration, ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if no secondary damage; may recur if driving patterns unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated emissions strategy, full diagnostics, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for fleet operators; eliminates repeat DPF issues and protects commercial uptime

problems.recommendation: For Vivaro used on short urban routes, forced regeneration may buy time. For revenue-critical commercial use, a rebuilt engine with emissions calibration included offers predictable costs and minimises future downtime risk.
problems.cta: Compare DPF-compliant rebuilt engine prices for your Vauxhall Vivaro

problems.h4: Vauxhall Vivaro Turbocharger Failure - Oil Seal Leaks & Power Loss on High-Mileage Commercial Use
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Vivaro Panel Van, Crew Van, Platform Cab (2001-2024, F9Q, G9U, M9R, R9M, DW10)
problems.rootCause: Commercial high-load use accelerates turbo bearing wear; oil seal failure causes blue smoke, power loss and can contaminate intercooler or DPF.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean/replace actuator, check hoses, reset adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses control issue; does not fix internal bearing or seal wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new/reconditioned turbo, replace oil/feed lines, flush system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; ensure intercooler cleaned to prevent repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo, full system flush, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage commercial Vivaro; eliminates risk of secondary engine damage

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure is caught early with no oil contamination of intercooler/DPF, turbo replacement is cost-effective. For Vivaro over 80k miles used commercially, a rebuilt engine with warranty offers better protection against cascading failures and business downtime.
problems.cta: Get quotes for turbo-upgraded rebuilt Vauxhall Vivaro engines

problems.h4: Vauxhall Vivaro AdBlue System Fault - Emissions Compliance Issues on Mk3 PSA-Sourced Engines
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Vivaro Panel Van, Crew Van, Combi (2019-2024, DV5504, DV5508, DW10504, DW10508)
problems.rootCause: AdBlue injector crystallisation or NOx sensor failure triggers emissions fault, limiting power and risking MOT failure on commercial vehicles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, injector clean, tank refill, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; does not address hardware faults; may recur quickly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace injector/sensor, recalibrate system, emissions test
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses specific fault but not root cause if engine management strategy flawed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with AdBlue calibration included, full diagnostics, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for fleet compliance; ensures emissions certification and minimises repeat fault risk

problems.recommendation: For Mk3 Vivaro used in ULEZ/CAZ zones, AdBlue system faults risk operational restrictions. A rebuilt engine with emissions calibration included offers predictable compliance and protects commercial revenue streams.
problems.cta: Compare AdBlue-compliant rebuilt engine prices for your Mk3 Vauxhall Vivaro

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Vauxhall Vivaro
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain, EGR, DPF, turbo and AdBlue failures are common on high-mileage Vivaro commercial use, but EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Vauxhall Vivaro Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Vauxhall Vivaro and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.descriptor: Diesel power has defined the Vivaro across all three generations, with Renault-sourced units (Mk1/Mk2) and PSA-sourced engines (Mk3) dominating UK commercial replacement enquiries.

engineYears.h2: Vauxhall Vivaro Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Vauxhall Vivaro's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Mk1 Launch: F9Q 1.9 dCi & G9U 2.5 dCi Debut
years.description: The original Vivaro launched with Renault-sourced belt-driven diesels, establishing the Panel/Crew Van commercial formula.
keyChanges: Vivaro replaces earlier Vauxhall Arena/Toyota-based models
F9Q 1.9 dCi introduced as entry diesel; G9U 2.5 dCi for heavy-duty variants
Panel Van, Crew Van and early Platform Cab body styles available
knownFor: Robust belt-driven design but interference engine requiring timing belt vigilance
Swirl flap actuators prone to sticking on high-mileage commercial use
Strong residual values for well-maintained early examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2006 Vauxhall Vivaro replacement engines
years.ticker: F9Q timing belt replacement cost • G9U turbo seal leak fix • Vivaro 2001-2006 engine swap compatibility

years.description: The 2006 update introduced the chain-driven M9R 2.0 dCi, becoming the Vivaro's most common replacement engine due to tensioner issues.
keyChanges: M9R 2.0 dCi replaces F9Q as mainstream diesel option
Updated interior and exterior styling for Mk1 facelift
Timing chain design introduced (with known tensioner weakness)
mainEngines: M9R708 - 2.0L diesel higher output (Platform Cab, heavy-duty variants)
knownFor: M9R timing chain tensioner failures between 60k-100k miles drive replacement demand
Strong commercial uptake; high-mileage examples common on UK roads
Aftermarket tensioner upgrades available for preventative maintenance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2014 Vauxhall Vivaro replacement engines
years.ticker: M9R timing chain rattle fix • Vivaro 2.0 dCi tensioner replacement cost • High-mileage commercial engine swap

years.preview: Mk2 Launch: R9M Updated 2.0 dCi & Emissions Focus
years.description: The all-new Mk2 platform brought the updated R9M 2.0 dCi with improved emissions hardware but retained timing chain design requiring verification.
keyChanges: New generation platform with updated chassis and body styling
R9M 2.0 dCi replaces M9R with revised emissions systems
Combi passenger variant expanded; Platform Cab continues
knownFor: EGR cooler clogging and DPF regeneration issues on urban commercial use
Improved NVH and fuel economy over Mk1 but complexity drives replacement costs
Strong fleet adoption; high-volume replacement enquiries from tradespeople
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2019 Vauxhall Vivaro replacement engines
years.ticker: R9M EGR cooler replacement • Vivaro DPF forced regen cost • Mk2 2.0 dCi engine swap compatibility

years.description: The PSA Group acquisition brought BlueHDi engines to Vivaro, requiring AdBlue calibration for emissions compliance on replacement.
keyChanges: Vivaro shares platform with Peugeot Expert/Citroën Dispatch
PSA-sourced DV5 1.6L and DW10 2.0L BlueHDi engines replace Renault units
AdBlue system mandatory for Euro 6 compliance; calibration required on fitment
knownFor: AdBlue injector crystallisation and NOx sensor faults trigger limp mode
Improved refinement but emissions complexity increases replacement calibration requirements
ULEZ/CAZ compliance drives commercial replacement decisions in urban areas
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2024 Vauxhall Vivaro replacement engines
years.ticker: Mk3 AdBlue calibration cost • DW10 injector seal replacement • Vivaro 2019+ emissions compliance engine swap

faq.h2: Vauxhall Vivaro Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Vauxhall Vivaro engine prices range from £1,200 for early 1.9L units to £3,650 for late 2.0L BlueHDi variants, supply only. Supply & fit adds £400-£850 depending on location and variant.
keyPoints: Prices vary by engine code, variant and supplier network
Rebuilt units include upgraded components addressing known failures
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on network engines
Get tailored quotes by entering your registration or engine code
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Vauxhall Vivaro engine prices for your variant

items.question: What is the most common Vauxhall Vivaro engine problem?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner failure on M9R/R9M 2.0 dCi engines is the single most reported issue, typically occurring between 60,000-100,000 miles on high-mileage commercial Vivaro use.
keyPoints: Rattling noise on cold start indicates tensioner wear
Ignoring symptoms risks valve damage and catastrophic failure
Upgraded tensioner kits available for preventative replacement
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components as standard
items.cta: Get quotes for M9R/R9M Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement

items.question: How do I find my Vauxhall Vivaro engine code?
items.answer: Check your V5C logbook (section D.2), the engine bay sticker, or use our free registration lookup to instantly identify your Vivaro's exact engine code and compatible replacements.
keyPoints: Common codes: F9Q, G9U, M9R, R9M, DV5, DW10
Code determines compatibility and emissions calibration requirements
Enter reg above for instant DVLA-verified identification
Specialists require code for accurate quoting and warranty validation
items.cta: Use our free Vauxhall Vivaro engine code lookup tool

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Vauxhall Vivaro engine?
items.answer: For commercial Vivaro generating revenue, replacement with a warrantied rebuilt engine usually offers better value than repair when costs exceed 60% of replacement price, factoring in downtime.
keyPoints: Downtime costs often outweigh repair savings for tradespeople
Rebuilt engines include warranty protecting business continuity
Supply & fit options minimise operational disruption
Compare quotes to assess best value for your specific variant
items.cta: Calculate repair vs replace value for your Vauxhall Vivaro

items.question: What warranty comes with a Vauxhall Vivaro replacement engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt and reconditioned Vauxhall Vivaro engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with extended options available for fleet accounts.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK garage
Commercial-use terms available for fleet operators
Confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before purchase
Documentation provided for insurance and compliance records
items.cta: Compare warranty terms across Vauxhall Vivaro engine suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Vauxhall Vivaro?
items.answer: Engine codes must match original specifications or be verified compatible for emissions, ECU coding and insurance compliance; use our compatibility tool before ordering to avoid costly mistakes.
keyPoints: Renault-sourced (Mk1/Mk2) and PSA-sourced (Mk3) engines are not interchangeable
AdBlue calibration required for Mk3 BlueHDi fitment
ECU recoding may be needed for automatic transmission variants
Specialists can verify compatibility using your registration
items.cta: Verify engine compatibility for your Vauxhall Vivaro before ordering

items.question: How long does Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical supply & fit completion is 2-3 working days from order confirmation, with express slots available at commercial garages nationwide for urgent fleet requirements.
keyPoints: Engine dispatch usually within 24 hours of order
Fitting time varies by variant complexity and garage workload
Priority scheduling available for fleet account holders
Mobile fitting options at commercial depots in major cities
items.cta: Book express Vauxhall Vivaro engine fitting at a commercial garage near you

items.question: Do you offer mobile Vauxhall Vivaro engine fitting?
items.answer: Mobile fitting is available at commercial depots and business locations in major UK cities; enter your postcode during quote request to check local availability and downtime estimates.
keyPoints: Service covers Panel Van, Crew Van and Combi variants
Van-bay equipped mobile units for complex engine swaps
Ideal for fleet operators minimising vehicle collection/delivery
Confirm location suitability and access requirements when booking
items.cta: Check mobile fitting availability for your Vauxhall Vivaro location

items.question: What's the difference between used, reconditioned and remanufactured Vivaro engines?
items.answer: Used engines are low-mileage donor units with limited warranty; reconditioned engines are stripped and rebuilt with replaced wear parts; remanufactured units meet original factory specs with new components.
keyPoints: Reconditioned offers best balance of cost and warranty for commercial use
Remanufactured recommended for late-model Mk3 emissions compliance
Used engines suitable for older Mk1 where budget is primary concern
All network options include minimum 12-month warranty coverage
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs remanufactured Vauxhall Vivaro engine options

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Vauxhall Vivaro insurance?
items.answer: Most insurers accept warrantied like-for-like replacement engines; provide documentation from EngineMarket specialists to maintain commercial policy validity and payload compliance.
keyPoints: Notify insurer of engine replacement to avoid claim complications
Warranty documentation supports insurance acceptance
Ensure replacement maintains original GVW and payload ratings
Specialists can provide compliance certificates for fleet records
items.cta: Get insurance-compliant Vauxhall Vivaro engine replacement quotes
